summary: Advanced Microeconomic Theory (3rd Edition) by Geoffrey A. Jehle and Philip J. Reny is a graduate-level textbook providing rigorous coverage of modern microeconomic theory, including consumer theory, firm theory, general equilibrium, game theory, information economics, and mechanism design. The third edition expands coverage of contingent commodities, social choice theorems, Bayesian games, and efficient mechanism design, supported by extensive mathematical appendices and over fifty new exercises.
bullets: Covers core microeconomic theory: consumer theory, firm theory, partial and general equilibrium, social choice, game theory, information economics, and auctions/mechanism design

New in the 3rd edition: general equilibrium with contingent commodities, simplified proof of Arrow's theorem, full development of the Gibbard-Satterthwaite theorem, and extensive Bayesian games treatment
Chapter 9 provides an integrated presentation of mechanism design in quasi-linear utility, private-values environments, including VCG mechanisms and revenue equivalence
Includes two full chapters of mathematical appendices covering set theory, topology, calculus, and optimization (Lagrangian and Kuhn-Tucker methods)
Targeted at Masters students, beginning PhD students, and advanced undergraduates, with over fifty new exercises and hints/answers provided
